title: Message Authentication Codes and HMAC · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Replay Window
summary: A webhook signs concatenated fields with raw SHA-256, reuses an encryption key, compares hex strings, and accepts the same request repeatedly. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the replay window increment in replay-resistant HMAC request authenticator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enforce bounded freshness and one-time request identity after authenticating the request, with durable duplicate handling.
Define exact method, target, selected headers, body bytes, timestamp, key ID, and version canonicalization before MAC verification.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Replay Window · Theory Crypto Go Replay Window
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 7085 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify a purpose-separated canonical HMAC request with freshness and durable replay rejection; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enforce bounded freshness and one-time request identity after authenticating the request, with durable duplicate handling.
